architecture.summary: The repository is composed of three loosely coupled Gradio applications that form a user-managed pipeline: decompose an image into layers, edit selected layers, then recombine the result.
traits: Shallow file layout with script entry points instead of package modules
Workflow coupling through files and user actions instead of direct imports
Heavy reliance on external pretrained model pipelines
Export-oriented design for downstream creative tooling

components.name: Main Decomposition App
responsibilities: Accept image and inference controls from the Gradio UI
Create PPTX, ZIP, and PSD export artifacts

components.name: Layer Combiner
responsibilities: Normalize them to a common height

primary_path: Gradio UI collects image, prompts, layer count, seed, and inference settings
infer() runs the layered decomposition pipeline
Layer images are materialized as temporary PNG outputs
Export helpers build PPTX, ZIP, and PSD artifacts
User optionally edits one or more layers in src/tool/edit_rgba_image.py

inputs: Single RGBA layer plus edit prompt for editing
intermediate_artifacts: Temporary PNG, PPTX, ZIP, and PSD files

internal: Minimal direct code-level integration across scripts
Shared workflow semantics defined by file outputs and user actions

strengths: Export formats make the repo useful beyond pure model demos
risks: Missing dependency manifest makes environment recreation fragile
Snapshot suggests missing assets referenced by the code
CUDA and bfloat16 assumptions may fail on unsupported hardware
Manual file handoff between apps can introduce user error
